message:
feat(fp-io): schema-qualified DROP TABLE in write_sql Replace branch (br-hxob / fd90.16)

Closes br-frankenpandas-hxob (fd90.16). Plugs the last gap in fd90.15: the SqlIfExists::Replace path's DROP TABLE was using the free quote_sql_ident helper directly, bypassing both conn.quote_identifier (so backend dialect overrides like MySQL backticks didn't take effect on DROP) and the schema qualifier (so multi-schema backends would drop the wrong table when options.schema was set).

- New private helper sql_drop_table_query_in_schema(conn, table, schema): mirrors the CREATE / INSERT / SELECT in_schema helpers. Routes through conn.quote_identifier; applies schema qualification when supports_schemas() is true; silently ignores schema on backends that report false (SQLite); validates schema name against the same alphanumeric+underscore policy.
- write_sql_with_options Replace branch now calls the helper. The redundant 'let schema = options.schema.as_deref()' block from the CREATE/INSERT region merged into a single binding hoisted above the if_exists match.

3 new unit tests:
- bare DROP on non-multi-schema stub (schema=None and schema=Some both yield 'DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "users"').
- qualified DROP on multi-schema stub (schema=Some yields 'DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "analytics"."users"').
- write_sql Replace round-trip on SQLite with schema=Some silently ignored — pre-existing rows dropped, new rows land in the bare table.

rch: 271 fp-io tests pass (268 prior + 3 new), cargo clippy -p fp-io --all-targets -D warnings clean, ubs Critical=0 exit 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
